evals.prompt: I want to build a CLI tool that helps developers manage their dotfiles across multiple machines. It should support symlinking, backup, and restore. Let's plan this out.
evals.expected_output: Claude should enter discovery phase, ask questions one at a time about the project (target platforms, language preferences, existing dotfile setups), suggest options with trade-offs, and eventually propose drafting a proposal. Should NOT dump a full spec immediately.

evals.prompt: I was working on a project last week and had to stop. There should be a SPEC.md and PROGRESS.md in the workspace. Can you pick up where I left off?
evals.expected_output: Claude should detect the resumption scenario, read both SPEC.md and PROGRESS.md, identify that M2.3 (Sidebar session list) is the next sub-milestone, summarize the current state, and ask the user if they want to continue. Should NOT restart discovery or overwrite existing files. When user says continue, Claude should ACTUALLY BUILD the code for M2.3 — create files, write code, produce working output.

files.content:
# Task Manager API - Specification

## Overview

A REST API for personal task management.

| Aspect             | Description                                       |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- |
| **Project Name**   | task-manager-api                                  |
| **Users**          | Single developer, personal use                    |
| **User Goal**      | Manage tasks via API                              |
| **Developer Goal** | Learn API design patterns                         |

## Execution Plan

| Phase | Milestone                 | Description                                       |
| ----- | ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| M0    | Project Setup             | Infrastructure, dependencies, basic server        |
| M1    | Task CRUD                 | Create, read, update, delete tasks                |
| M2    | Authentication            | JWT-based auth for the API                        |

## Milestones

> **⛔ STOP RULE:** Complete ONE sub-milestone, then STOP and wait for user review.

### M0 - Project Setup

#### M0.1 - Initialize project structure
- [x] Initialize Node.js project with TypeScript
- [x] Create `src/` folder structure
- [x] Create `.gitignore` and `README.md`
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

#### M0.2 - Basic server
- [x] Create `src/server.ts` with Express app
- [x] Add health check endpoint (`GET /health`)
- [x] Configure SQLite with better-sqlite3
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

---

### M1 - Task CRUD

#### M1.1 - Task model and creation
- [x] Create `src/models/task.ts` with Task interface
- [x] Create migration for tasks table
- [x] Implement `POST /tasks` endpoint
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

#### M1.2 - Read and list tasks
- [ ] Implement `GET /tasks` with pagination
- [ ] Implement `GET /tasks/:id`
- [ ] Return 404 for invalid IDs
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

#### M1.3 - Update and delete
- [ ] Implement `PUT /tasks/:id`
- [ ] Implement `DELETE /tasks/:id`
- [ ] Return 400 for missing required fields
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

---

### M2 - Authentication

#### M2.1 - User model
- [ ] Create `src/models/user.ts`
- [ ] Create migration for users table
- [ ] Implement `POST /register` endpoint
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

#### M2.2 - Login and JWT
- [ ] Implement `POST /login` returning JWT
- [ ] Create `src/middleware/auth.ts` for token validation
- [ ] Protect task endpoints with auth middleware
<!-- ⛔ STOP -->

# Progress

## Completed: M0 - Project Setup

- [x] M0.1: completed - project structure initialized
- [x] M0.2: completed - Express server with health check and SQLite

## Current: M1 - Task CRUD

- [x] M1.1: completed - Task model, migration, POST endpoint
- [ ] M1.2: not started - Read and list tasks

## Notes
- Used better-sqlite3 over sql.js for performance
- Added nodemon for dev workflow
- TypeScript strict mode enabled

evals.prompt: I just need you to build me a simple markdown blog generator in Python. Don't overthink it — just read markdown files from a folder and output HTML. Let's go.
evals.expected_output: Claude should recognize the fast-builder mode. Should briefly offer a minimal spec (overview + milestones + tasks), then respect the user's choice. If user says skip, proceed to BUILD immediately — actually create Python files, write the code, produce a working tool. Must NOT force the full discovery/proposal workflow. Must NOT only plan without building.

evals.prompt: The proposal looks good, let's generate the spec.
evals.expected_output: Claude should generate a SPEC.md with: (1) Overview section with summary table, (2) Execution Plan table showing all milestones, (3) Milestones broken into sub-milestones with granular file-level tasks as checkboxes, (4) ⛔ STOP comments after each sub-milestone, (5) Architecture Notes where relevant. Should also create initial PROGRESS.md. Should NOT use the old flat milestone format without sub-milestones.
